name: Pumpkin Marble Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the gingersnap crumbs, pecans, and melted margarine. Press the mixture onto the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 10 minutes.
3. In a large bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, 1/2 cup sugar, and vanilla. Mix well on medium speed with an electric mixer until fully blended.
4. Add eggs one at a time, mixing thoroughly after each addition. Set aside.
5. Reserve half of this batter for later use.
6. In the remaining bowl, mix together the remaining sugar, canned pumpkin, and spices (cinnamon and nutmeg).
7. Alternately layer the reserved cream cheese batter and the pumpkin mixture over the crust in the springform pan. Use a knife to cut through the batters several times for a marble effect.
8. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 55 minutes.
9. Loosen the cake from the rim of the pan, then cool before removing the rim of the pan and allowing it to chill completely.
